page: Microsoft Azure M416s_10_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MSv2 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 416 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture using Intel processors. It is equipped with 9496.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 22.83 GB per vCPU.

The server includes 4194 GB of local storage and is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput storage configurations. It does not feature any integrated GPUs and includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its massive memory capacity and high dedicated core count, this server represents a high-density hardware class designed for enterprise-scale workloads. It is qualitatively structured for memory-intensive applications where resource consolidation is critical. Appropriate workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise relational database management systems, massive data warehousing, and heavy transactional workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure M416s_10_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MSv2 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 416 dedicated x86_64 Intel vCPUs and an extensive memory capacity of 9496.0 GB, providing approximately 22.83 GB of RAM per core. Designed for high-density enterprise workloads, this instance includes 4194 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, though it contains no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The qualitative cost profile reflects its massive resource density, making it a specialized option for consolidating large-scale workloads. This server is engineered for memory-intensive applications, including massive in-memory databases, enterprise-grade relational databases, and large-scale data warehousing.
